title: Money and Desire Are Not at War in Me
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the split between financial ambition and personal desire
excerpt: A short affirmation for women who have absorbed the rule that claiming money must come at the cost of something else. Said daily, it works against the split.

practice.whenToUse: Use when financial ambition and personal desire feel like they belong to different women, or when claiming one triggers guilt about the other.

content.text: A daily affirmation for the split. Many women absorb a cultural rule that says financial power and desire belong in different women — or that having one costs the other. This affirmation works against that rule, one repetition at a time.
Money and desire are not at war in me. I claim both.

Say it three times in the morning, hand on heart. Say it again when the split appears — when claiming money triggers guilt, or when ambition feels like it's costing something. The rule was absorbed over decades; the affirmation works against it one day at a time.
